votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the recursive helper functions, string manipulations, and the precondition/specification logic.
The Python code directly translates the Coq recursive functions and logic, maintaining the exact same behavior for all concrete inputs.
The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions, helper functions, and specifications, maintaining the exact same logic and structure.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions. IsBalanced_aux, remove_spaces, ForallChars, and separate_paren_groups_aux all follow the same recursive logic with identical base cases and branching conditions. The string manipulation (using list reversal and join) correctly mirrors Coq's string_of_list_ascii and List.rev. The precondition and specification functions check the same properties and compare against the same implementation result.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions. IsBalanced_aux, remove_spaces, ForallChars, and separate_paren_groups_aux all follow the same recursive structure and logic as their Coq counterparts. The only difference is that Python uses list prepending with [h] + current instead of cons, but since the result is reversed when converting to string, this produces the same output. The precondition and specification functions also match exactly in their concrete behavior.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions. IsBalanced_aux, remove_spaces, ForallChars, and separate_paren_groups_aux all follow the same recursive structure and logic as their Coq counterparts. The main implementation separate_paren_groups_impl processes the input identically, and the pre/spec functions return the same boolean/equality judgments for concrete inputs.

Python mirrors Coq logic for IsBalanced, remove_spaces, ForallChars, and separate_paren_groups_aux/impl, including handling of parentheses, spaces, other chars, group flushing when count returns to zero, and edge cases like unmatched right parens and leftover current. Outputs match on all inputs.

Python mirrors Coq logic for balance checking, space removal, character checks, and group separation, including edge cases (unmatched ')' returns current acc). For all inputs, it produces the same outputs and pre/spec judgments.
Python mirrors the Coq logic for balancing, space removal, character checks, and group separation, so it yields the same outputs on all inputs.
